title: Define a GatewayClass
description:
## Define a GatewayClass

In Kubernetes Gateway API, a `GatewayClass` is a cluster-scoped resource that acts as a template for provisioning `Gateway` instances. It defines the underlying controller implementation (such as Envoy, Traefik, or Istio) that will manage the network infrastructure.

**Your task:**

Create a cluster-scoped `GatewayClass` named `shared-gateway-class` that is configured with:
- `controllerName` set to `example.com/gateway-controller`

```bash
# Verify your progress:
kubectl get gatewayclass shared-gateway-class
```

hints.title: GatewayClass template
hints.body: GatewayClass is a cluster-scoped resource. A basic definition requires apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1, kind: GatewayClass, and spec.controllerName.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: GatewayClass
metadata:
  name: shared-gateway-class
spec:
  controllerName: example.com/gateway-controller
EOF

validation.description: Check that the GatewayClass shared-gateway-class exists and has the correct controllerName
